chapter_title.en: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings

parts.text: 544 - وَقَالَ صَفْوَانُ بْنُ يَحْيَى لِأَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ: بَلَغَنِي أَنَّ اَلْمُؤْمِنَ إِذَا أَتَاهُ اَلزَّائِرُ آنَسَ بِهِ فَإِذَا اِنْصَرَفَ عَنْهُ اِسْتَوْحَشَ فَقَالَ

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.544 - Safwan ibn Yahya said to Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as):
"I have heard that when a believer is visited by someone (at their grave), they are comforted by their presence, but when the visitor leaves, they feel lonely."
Imam (as) replied: "They do not feel lonely."
